authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Smith River Main Stem
reaches.fromDesc: The mouth (Pacific Ocean near the town of Smith River)
reaches.toDesc: Confluence of the Middle and South (via North) Forks near Gasquet

reaches.name: Middle Fork Smith River (mouth to Patrick Creek)
reaches.fromDesc: The mouth (confluence with North Fork near Gasquet, forming the main stem)

reaches.name: Middle Fork Smith River (above Patrick Creek)
reaches.toDesc: Headwaters near the Oregon border (Siskiyou Wilderness)

reaches.name: South Fork Smith River (mouth to Jones Creek)
reaches.fromDesc: Approximately 1,000 feet above the mouth, at the County Road (George Tryon) bridge, and Craigs Creek

reaches.name: South Fork Smith River (George Tryon bridge to Craigs Creek)
South Fork Smith River (above Jones Creek)
North Fork Smith River (mouth to Stony Creek)
reaches.fromDesc: The mouth (confluence with Middle Fork near Gasquet)

reaches.name: North Fork Smith River (above Stony Creek)
reaches.toDesc: Headwaters near the Oregon border (North Fork Smith River Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(105) — Smith River (Del Norte Co.)
sources.quotedText: (105) Smith River (Del Norte Co.). Yearly limits apply for entire river. Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions also apply.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Daily bag/possession limits for hatchery trout, hatchery steelhead, and Cutthroat Trout apply per reach, but the annual limit of no more than 5 wild Chinook Salmon over 22 inches total length is a combined yearly limit for the entire Smith River (main stem plus North, Middle, and South Forks), not a separate per-reach allowance.

regulations.humanSummary: Yearly limits (e.g., the wild Chinook Salmon annual limit) apply river-wide across the entire Smith River, not per reach
regulations.verbatimText: Yearly limits apply for entire river.

parameters.note: Additional low-flow fishing closures under 14 CCR §8.00(a) can close all or part of the Smith River and its forks when streamflow drops below specified thresholds; anglers should check current CDFW low-flow closure status before fishing.

regulations.humanSummary: Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions also apply and may close fishing when river flows are low
regulations.verbatimText: Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions also apply.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Main stem open fourth Saturday in May through April 30
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Apr. 30.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through Aug. 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Fourth Sat. in May through Aug. 31: only artificial lures with barbless hooks
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Aug. 31.

parameters.note: Only barbless hooks may be used from Sep. 1 through Apr. 30 (bait permitted if fished with a barbless hook).

regulations.humanSummary: Sep. 1 through Apr. 30: only barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Sep. 1 through Apr. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day; 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.
